message:
feat(string): port sys_signame BSD short signal-name array (bd-vqevs)

Adds the BSD-conventional `const char *const sys_signame[NSIG]`
array — short uppercase signal names without the "SIG" prefix,
indexed by signal number. Used by killall(1), kill(1), trap(1),
and other tools that prefer the abbreviated form ("HUP" rather
than "Hangup"). Some BSD-derived ports of Linux libraries (libbsd)
provide this for compatibility with code that grew up on
NetBSD/FreeBSD.

The 65-entry layout matches the previously-shipped sys_siglist:

- Index 0 is empty.
- Indices 1..31 hold the standard short names ("HUP", "INT",
  "QUIT", "ILL", "TRAP", "ABRT", "BUS", "FPE", "KILL", "USR1",
  "SEGV", "USR2", "PIPE", "ALRM", "TERM", "STKFLT", "CHLD",
  "CONT", "STOP", "TSTP", "TTIN", "TTOU", "URG", "XCPU", "XFSZ",
  "VTALRM", "PROF", "WINCH", "IO", "PWR", "SYS").
- Indices 32..64 cover the realtime range and share a generic
  "RT" placeholder; callers wanting precise short forms (e.g.
  "RTMIN+3") should call `sigabbrev_np` instead.

Implementation: re-uses the `repr(transparent)` SysSigList
wrapper introduced for sys_siglist (so the C ABI remains a bare
`const char *sys_signame[NSIG]`), with per-signal short-name
byte literals defined alongside the existing description literals.

Tests: 5 abi integration tests covering array length (65),
sys_signame[0] is empty, well-known short names for HUP/INT/KILL/
SEGV/TERM/CHLD/WINCH/SYS, all 33 realtime slots use the "RT"
placeholder, and sys_signame[n] matches sigabbrev_np(n) for the
standard signals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-n3gtm","title":"PORT: sys_siglist (deprecated glibc signal-description array)","description":"Port the deprecated-but-widely-referenced glibc symbol `const char *const sys_siglist[NSIG]` — an array of human-readable signal descriptions indexed by signal number. NSIG on Linux x86_64 is 65 (signals 0..64); entries 0 and 32..63 are typically empty/realtime placeholders, 1..31 cover the standard POSIX/Linux signals (Hangup, Interrupt, Quit, Illegal instruction, etc). Many older programs reference sys_siglist directly instead of calling strsignal(); without it, they fail at link time. Implementation: a `pub static sys_siglist: [*const c_char; NSIG]` exported as a no_mangle symbol, populated at compile time from byte-string literals matching the existing signal_name() table in string_abi. Tests verify the array is the right length, well-known indices map to expected descriptions, and indexing past NSIG is bounded.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:37:13.855513667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:37:13.855513667Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Tests verify the array is the right length, well-known indices map to expected descriptions, and indexing past NSIG is bounded.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:37:13.855513667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:41:33.956193519Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:41:33.954220402Z","close_reason":"shipped: sys_siglist[NSIG] deprecated signal-description array via repr(transparent) SysSigList newtype + 5 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-voxzi","title":"PORT: strnstr (BSD bounded substring search)","description":"BSD strnstr(haystack, needle, n) — like strstr but searches at most n bytes of haystack, returning a pointer to the first occurrence of needle (still NUL-terminated) or NULL if not found. Used by many BSD ports; without it programs that depend on it will not link against frankenlibc. Currently missing from frankenlibc-abi/string_abi.rs. Ship core::string::str::strnstr (returning Option<usize>) plus an abi shim that handles NULL pointer and casts to char*.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T15:14:29.383398661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T15:18:35.591905897Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T15:18:35.590011542Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::string::str::strnstr + string_abi::strnstr; 12+10 tests; bound-respecting via core_strnlen; clippy-clean","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-vq1g","title":"D.1: (SUPERSEDED) — folded into parent bd-9dq5","description":"## Goal
Implement the request-tracking infrastructure for async DNS and the gai_error/gai_cancel functions.

## Design

### Request Registry
A global mutex-protected HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest> maps gaicb pointer addresses to request state. The gaicb struct (defined by POSIX) contains:
- ar_name: hostname to resolve
- ar_service: service name/port
- ar_request: hints (addrinfo struct)
- ar_result: output addrinfo list (written on completion)

### gai_error(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req address in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_SYSTEM (unknown request)
3. Return state: EAI_INPROGRESS (still running), 0 (completed successfully), EAI_CANCELLED, or the error code from getaddrinfo

### gai_cancel(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_NOCANCEL
3. If already completed: return EAI_ALLDONE
4. If in progress: set CANCELLED flag, return EAI_CANCELLED (or EAI_NOTCANCELED if thread already started resolution)

### State Constants
- EAI_INPROGRESS = -100 (glibc value)
- EAI_CANCELLED = -101
- EAI_ALLDONE = -103
- EAI_NOTCANCELED = -102

## Implementation
File: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs (near existing gai_* functions)
- Add AsyncDnsRequest struct
- Add static ASYNC_DNS_REGISTRY: LazyLock<Mutex<HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest>>>
- Replace gai_error body
- Replace gai_cancel body

## Testing
- gai_error on unknown request returns EAI_SYSTEM
- gai_cancel on unknown request returns EAI_NOCANCEL
- Register a fake completed request, gai_error returns 0
- Register a fake in-progress request, gai_cancel returns EAI_CANCELLED

## Acceptance Criteria
State machine is correct. Registry tracks requests. Tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:44.987634072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.338086567Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.337881268Z","close_reason":"Superseded: Track D redesigned as synchronous-only. No request registry or futex wait needed. All logic fits in parent bd-9dq5.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","resolv","stub-elimination"]}

Entries 1..31 are "HUP", "INT", "QUIT", "ILL", "TRAP", "ABRT", "BUS", "FPE", "KILL", "USR1", "SEGV", "USR2", "PIPE", "ALRM", "TERM", "STKFLT", "CHLD", "CONT", "STOP", "TSTP", "TTIN", "TTOU", "URG", "XCPU", "XFSZ", "VTALRM", "PROF", "WINCH", "IO", "PWR", "SYS"; entry 0 is empty; entries 32..64 are realtime placeholders. NetBSD/FreeBSD callers expect this symbol; some Linux ports (libbsd) provide it. Implementation: same repr(transparent) SysSigList wrapper pattern as the recently-shipped sys_siglist, but holding the abbreviated name strings instead of the descriptions. Tests verify length, well-known indices, and matching shape against sigabbrev_np.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:45:13.092404101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:45:13.092404101Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-w1bd","title":"syscall: timer_create sigevent thread-id veneer","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T10:09:14.384383086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T10:57:38.455091544Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T10:57:38.452599939Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -5582,6 +5582,128 @@ pub static sys_siglist: SysSigList = SysSigList([
     SIG_DESC_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 64
 ]);
 
+// Per-signal short name bytes used by `sys_signame` — uppercase
+// without the "SIG" prefix, matching the BSD convention used by
+// killall(1), kill(1), and other signal-name tools.
+const SIG_NAME_EMPTY: &[u8] = b"\0";
+const SIG_NAME_HUP: &[u8] = b"HUP\0";
+const SIG_NAME_INT: &[u8] = b"INT\0";
+const SIG_NAME_QUIT: &[u8] = b"QUIT\0";
+const SIG_NAME_ILL: &[u8] = b"ILL\0";
+const SIG_NAME_TRAP: &[u8] = b"TRAP\0";
+const SIG_NAME_ABRT: &[u8] = b"ABRT\0";
+const SIG_NAME_BUS: &[u8] = b"BUS\0";
+const SIG_NAME_FPE: &[u8] = b"FPE\0";
+const SIG_NAME_KILL: &[u8] = b"KILL\0";
+const SIG_NAME_USR1: &[u8] = b"USR1\0";
+const SIG_NAME_SEGV: &[u8] = b"SEGV\0";
+const SIG_NAME_USR2: &[u8] = b"USR2\0";
+const SIG_NAME_PIPE: &[u8] = b"PIPE\0";
+const SIG_NAME_ALRM: &[u8] = b"ALRM\0";
+const SIG_NAME_TERM: &[u8] = b"TERM\0";
+const SIG_NAME_STKFLT: &[u8] = b"STKFLT\0";
+const SIG_NAME_CHLD: &[u8] = b"CHLD\0";
+const SIG_NAME_CONT: &[u8] = b"CONT\0";
+const SIG_NAME_STOP: &[u8] = b"STOP\0";
+const SIG_NAME_TSTP: &[u8] = b"TSTP\0";
+const SIG_NAME_TTIN: &[u8] = b"TTIN\0";
+const SIG_NAME_TTOU: &[u8] = b"TTOU\0";
+const SIG_NAME_URG: &[u8] = b"URG\0";
+const SIG_NAME_XCPU: &[u8] = b"XCPU\0";
+const SIG_NAME_XFSZ: &[u8] = b"XFSZ\0";
+const SIG_NAME_VTALRM: &[u8] = b"VTALRM\0";
+const SIG_NAME_PROF: &[u8] = b"PROF\0";
+const SIG_NAME_WINCH: &[u8] = b"WINCH\0";
+const SIG_NAME_IO: &[u8] = b"IO\0";
+const SIG_NAME_PWR: &[u8] = b"PWR\0";
+const SIG_NAME_SYS: &[u8] = b"SYS\0";
+const SIG_NAME_RT: &[u8] = b"RT\0";
+
+/// BSD `sys_signame[NSIG]` — array of short uppercase signal
+/// names (no `"SIG"` prefix), indexed by signal number. Used by
+/// killall(1), kill(1), and other signal-name tools that prefer
+/// the abbreviated form ("HUP" rather than "Hangup"). Some BSD-
+/// derived ports of Linux libraries (libbsd) provide this for
+/// compatibility.
+///
+/// `sys_signame[0]` is empty. Indices 32..=64 share a generic
+/// `"RT"` placeholder; callers wanting the full short form (e.g.
+/// `"RTMIN+3"`) should call `sigabbrev_np` instead.
+///
+/// The wrapper around the inner `[*const c_char; 65]` is
+/// `repr(transparent)`, so the symbol's ABI is identical to a
+/// bare C `const char *sys_signame[NSIG]`.
+#[allow(non_upper_case_globals)]
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub static sys_signame: SysSigList = SysSigList([
+    SIG_NAME_EMPTY.as_ptr() as *const c_char,  // 0
+    SIG_NAME_HUP.as_ptr() as *const c_char,    // 1 SIGHUP
+    SIG_NAME_INT.as_ptr() as *const c_char,    // 2 SIGINT
+    SIG_NAME_QUIT.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 3 SIGQUIT
+    SIG_NAME_ILL.as_ptr() as *const c_char,    // 4 SIGILL
+    SIG_NAME_TRAP.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 5 SIGTRAP
+    SIG_NAME_ABRT.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 6 SIGABRT
+    SIG_NAME_BUS.as_ptr() as *const c_char,    // 7 SIGBUS
+    SIG_NAME_FPE.as_ptr() as *const c_char,    // 8 SIGFPE
+    SIG_NAME_KILL.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 9 SIGKILL
+    SIG_NAME_USR1.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 10 SIGUSR1
+    SIG_NAME_SEGV.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 11 SIGSEGV
+    SIG_NAME_USR2.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 12 SIGUSR2
+    SIG_NAME_PIPE.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 13 SIGPIPE
+    SIG_NAME_ALRM.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 14 SIGALRM
+    SIG_NAME_TERM.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 15 SIGTERM
+    SIG_NAME_STKFLT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 16 SIGSTKFLT
+    SIG_NAME_CHLD.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 17 SIGCHLD
+    SIG_NAME_CONT.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 18 SIGCONT
+    SIG_NAME_STOP.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 19 SIGSTOP
+    SIG_NAME_TSTP.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 20 SIGTSTP
+    SIG_NAME_TTIN.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 21 SIGTTIN
+    SIG_NAME_TTOU.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 22 SIGTTOU
+    SIG_NAME_URG.as_ptr() as *const c_char,    // 23 SIGURG
+    SIG_NAME_XCPU.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 24 SIGXCPU
+    SIG_NAME_XFSZ.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 25 SIGXFSZ
+    SIG_NAME_VTALRM.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 26 SIGVTALRM
+    SIG_NAME_PROF.as_ptr() as *const c_char,   // 27 SIGPROF
+    SIG_NAME_WINCH.as_ptr() as *const c_char,  // 28 SIGWINCH
+    SIG_NAME_IO.as_ptr() as *const c_char,     // 29 SIGIO
+    SIG_NAME_PWR.as_ptr() as *const c_char,    // 30 SIGPWR
+    SIG_NAME_SYS.as_ptr() as *const c_char,    // 31 SIGSYS
+    // 32..=64: realtime signals — share a placeholder name.
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 32
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 33
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 34
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 35
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 36
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 37
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 38
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 39
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 40
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 41
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 42
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 43
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 44
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 45
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 46
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 47
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 48
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 49
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 50
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 51
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 52
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 53
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 54
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 55
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 56
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 57
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 58
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 59
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 60
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 61
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 62
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 63
+    SIG_NAME_RT.as_ptr() as *const c_char, // 64
+]);
+
 /// POSIX `psignal` — print a signal description to stderr.
 #[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
 pub unsafe extern "C" fn psignal(sig: c_int, s: *const c_char) {
